title: Full Stack Developer
tagline: I build end-to-end systems with Spring Boot and Angular — service contracts, data models, and deployment pipelines, not just the UI on top.
metaDescription: Annamalai Kathiresan — Senior Full Stack Developer. Java Spring Boot, REST APIs, Angular at Tata, Comcast, ETS, and Verizon.

about.prose: I've spent 5 years owning features from API contract to deployed UI. My backend work is Java and Spring Boot — REST endpoints, auth flows, database schemas — paired with Angular or React on the front. I'm comfortable in monoliths and microservices, and I think about deployment, observability, and failure modes, not just the happy path.

items.title: Tata Communications — Service Integration Layer
items.context: A licensed platform where Spring Boot services and Angular micro-frontends are released by different teams on different schedules.
items.built: Versioned API contracts between backend services and UI modules, integration test suites in the pipeline, and coordinated GitHub Actions releases with rollback paths.
items.outcome: Backend and frontend teams deploy independently without breaking cross-module dependencies — no more lockstep release trains.

items.title: Comcast — Batch Processing Backend
items.context: Bulk account operations pushed hundreds of requests through backend services that were built for one-at-a-time processing.
items.built: REST API integration for Bulk Invite and Bulk Modem Restart with request batching, partial-failure semantics, and idempotent retries so a failed row never corrupted the batch.
items.outcome: 75% faster onboarding for bulk operations with clean recovery from partial batch failures.

items.title: ETS — TOEFL Learn Backend & App
items.context: Mobile learning product needing auth, content delivery, and offline-tolerant sync across two platforms.
items.built: AWS Amplify backend with Cognito auth pools, a GraphQL content layer with fine-grained access rules, and the React Native client consuming it.
items.outcome: Single backend configuration serving both iOS and Android; auth and content APIs required zero platform-specific code.

items.title: Verizon — Metrics API & Dashboard
items.context: Ops dashboards fanned out to multiple slow REST endpoints on every page load.
items.built: Response caching and query batching on the API side, server-side pagination for heavy tables, and fixes for N+1 query patterns found through production profiling.
items.outcome: 30% load time reduction, with API response times stable under peak internal traffic.

highlights: Own API-to-UI delivery for Tata Communications licensed products
Design service contracts between Spring Boot backends and Angular micro-frontends
Run CI/CD pipelines covering backend and frontend test suites in one release flow
Delivered full-stack features for Verizon, ETS, and Comcast
Built batch-processing API integrations for high-traffic internal tools
Profiled and fixed N+1 query patterns and slow API responses in production

contact.message: Open to full-stack roles where I own a feature from database schema to deployed UI — especially Java + Angular systems.
